venice.golden_lion.note: Jia Zhangke's first Golden Lion — Chinese Sixth Generation auteur's first major Western festival prize. Still Life is a portrait of life affected by the Three Gorges Dam project. Jia later took Cannes Best Screenplay in 2013 (A Touch of Sin).
venice.golden_lion.source: 63rd Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.silver_lion_grand_jury.note: Mahamat-Saleh Haroun's first major Venice prize — Chad's first major win at the festival. Haroun later took the Cannes Jury Prize in 2010 (A Screaming Man).
venice.silver_lion_grand_jury.source: 63rd Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.silver_lion_best_director.note: Two-way tie. Alain Resnais (Private Fears in Public Places) shares Silver Lion for Best Director with Emanuele Crialese (Golden Door). Resnais was 84 at the time of the win — among the oldest Silver Lion winners. Crialese later took the Grand Jury Prize in 2011 (Terraferma). CSV co_winner flag missing on both rows due to multi-bullet-tie format — see known-issue venice-co-winner-flag-multi-bullet-tie-2026-05-04.
venice.silver_lion_best_director.source: 63rd Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.volpi_cup_best_actor.note: Ben Affleck's first Volpi Cup for Best Actor — for portraying TV's Superman George Reeves.
venice.volpi_cup_best_actor.source: 63rd Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.volpi_cup_best_actress.note: Helen Mirren's first Volpi Cup for Best Actress — she went on to win the Best Actress Oscar at the 79th Academy Awards (cer 79) for the same role. The Venice-to-Oscar acting pipeline played out cleanly. Same film also took Best Screenplay (Peter Morgan) at the same ceremony.
venice.volpi_cup_best_actress.source: 63rd Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.best_screenplay.note: Peter Morgan's first Venice Best Screenplay. The Queen went on to receive six Academy Award nominations at cer 79 including Best Picture and Best Original Screenplay.
venice.best_screenplay.source: 63rd Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.marcello_mastroianni_award.source: 63rd Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.lion_of_the_future.note: Belgian-Mongolian co-directed debut feature. Brosens and Woodworth's collaboration continued across multiple subsequent festival features.
venice.lion_of_the_future.source: 63rd Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.golden_lion.note: Ang Lee's second Golden Lion in three years — first was Brokeback Mountain (2005, outside scope). Lee becomes the first director in the 21st century to win two Golden Lions. Lust, Caution is an erotic spy thriller set in WWII-era Shanghai; the film received an NC-17 rating in the US, making it one of the highest-profile NC-17 releases since the rating was introduced.
venice.golden_lion.source: 64th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.silver_lion_best_director.note: Brian De Palma's first Silver Lion for Best Director — for his anti-Iraq War found-footage drama. The win was politically controversial; Redacted was based on the Mahmudiyah killings of 2006. (Note: silver_lion_grand_jury was not awarded at the 64th Venice — see era_override.)
venice.silver_lion_best_director.source: 64th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.special_jury_prize.note: Two-way tie. Todd Haynes's I'm Not There (Bob Dylan biopic with multiple actors playing Dylan, including Cate Blanchett who took Volpi Best Actress for the same film) shares with Abdellatif Kechiche's The Secret of the Grain. Both rows correctly flagged co_winner=true. Kechiche later won the Cannes Palme d'Or in 2013 (Blue Is the Warmest Colour, shared with the two lead actresses).
venice.special_jury_prize.source: 64th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.volpi_cup_best_actor.winner_film: The Assassination of Jesse James by the Coward Robert Ford
venice.volpi_cup_best_actor.note: Brad Pitt's first Volpi Cup for Best Actor.
venice.volpi_cup_best_actor.source: 64th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.volpi_cup_best_actress.note: Cate Blanchett's first Volpi Cup for Best Actress — for portraying a Bob Dylan-inspired character in Todd Haynes's experimental biopic. The cross-gender casting was widely discussed at the time. Blanchett went on to receive the Best Supporting Actress Oscar nomination at cer 80 for the same role, and won her second Volpi for Tár in 2022.
venice.volpi_cup_best_actress.source: 64th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.best_screenplay.note: Paul Laverty's first Venice Best Screenplay — Ken Loach's frequent collaborator. Laverty later took Venice Best Screenplay again in 2002 (Sweet Sixteen) — wait, 2002 came first; Laverty won Best Screenplay at Cannes 2002 for Sweet Sixteen and now this Venice 2007 prize.
venice.best_screenplay.source: 64th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.marcello_mastroianni_award.note: Hafsia Herzi's first major acting prize — same film took Special Jury Prize (shared, Kechiche).
venice.marcello_mastroianni_award.source: 64th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.lion_of_the_future.note: Mexican filmmaker Rodrigo Plá's debut feature.
venice.lion_of_the_future.source: 64th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.golden_lion.note: Darren Aronofsky's first Golden Lion — drama centred on Mickey Rourke's career-defining comeback as faded wrestler Randy 'The Ram' Robinson. Rourke went on to receive a Best Actor nomination at the 81st Academy Awards (cer 81). Aronofsky later directed Black Swan (2010 Venice opener — Mila Kunis took Mastroianni 2010).
venice.golden_lion.source: 65th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.silver_lion_best_director.note: Aleksei German Jr.'s first Silver Lion for Best Director — Russian filmmaker. (Note: silver_lion_grand_jury was not awarded at the 65th Venice — see era_override.)
venice.silver_lion_best_director.source: 65th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.special_jury_prize.note: Haile Gerima's first Venice Special Jury Prize — Ethiopian filmmaker's politically-charged drama. Same film also took Best Screenplay at the same ceremony — same-film cross-honour.
venice.special_jury_prize.source: 65th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.volpi_cup_best_actor.note: Silvio Orlando's first Volpi Cup for Best Actor — Italian veteran actor.
venice.volpi_cup_best_actor.source: 65th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.volpi_cup_best_actress.note: Dominique Blanc's first Volpi Cup for Best Actress.
venice.volpi_cup_best_actress.source: 65th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.best_screenplay.note: Haile Gerima takes Best Screenplay for the same film he won the Special Jury Prize for at the same ceremony — exceptional same-film cross-honour for an Ethiopian filmmaker.
venice.best_screenplay.source: 65th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.marcello_mastroianni_award.note: Jennifer Lawrence's first major international acting prize at age 18 — pre-The Hunger Games and pre-Winter's Bone Oscar nomination. Lawrence went on to win the Best Actress Oscar at the 85th Academy Awards (cer 85) for Silver Linings Playbook, and to be the youngest two-time Best Actress nominee at the time.
venice.marcello_mastroianni_award.source: 65th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.lion_of_the_future.source: 65th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.golden_lion.note: Samuel Maoz's debut feature Golden Lion — Israeli filmmaker's autobiographical drama about a tank crew during the 1982 Lebanon War. Maoz returned to Venice in 2017 with Foxtrot (Grand Jury Prize). Exceptional for a debut feature to take Venice's top prize.
venice.golden_lion.source: 66th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.silver_lion_grand_jury.note: Fatih Akin's first major Venice prize — German-Turkish filmmaker's comedy about a struggling restaurant in Hamburg.
venice.silver_lion_grand_jury.source: 66th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.silver_lion_best_director.note: Shirin Neshat becomes the first Iranian woman to win the Silver Lion for Best Director — Iranian-American visual artist's debut feature.
venice.silver_lion_best_director.source: 66th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.volpi_cup_best_actor.note: Colin Firth's first Volpi Cup for Best Actor — for fashion-designer-turned-filmmaker Tom Ford's debut feature. Firth went on to receive a Best Actor nomination at the 82nd Academy Awards (cer 82) for the same role, and won the Best Actor Oscar at cer 83 for The King's Speech.
venice.volpi_cup_best_actor.source: 66th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.volpi_cup_best_actress.note: Kseniya Rappoport's first Volpi Cup for Best Actress — Russian-Italian actress.
venice.volpi_cup_best_actress.source: 66th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.best_screenplay.note: Todd Solondz's first Venice Best Screenplay — sequel to his 1998 indie drama Happiness.
venice.best_screenplay.source: 66th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.marcello_mastroianni_award.source: 66th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.lion_of_the_future.note: Filipino debut feature wins Lion of the Future — Pepe Diokno's first festival-prize.
venice.lion_of_the_future.source: 66th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.golden_lion.note: Sofia Coppola's first Golden Lion — fourth feature, an autobiographical-leaning drama about a Hollywood actor's relationship with his daughter. Sofia is the second member of the Coppola family with a major festival top prize (after father Francis Ford Coppola won the Cannes Palme d'Or twice in 1974 + 1979). Coppola later won Cannes Best Director in 2017 (The Beguiled).
venice.golden_lion.source: 67th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.silver_lion_grand_jury.note: Jerzy Skolimowski's first Venice Grand Jury Prize — Polish auteur's drama. Same film took Volpi Cup for Best Actor (Vincent Gallo) at the same ceremony — same-film cross-honour. Skolimowski later took Cannes Jury Prize in 2022 (EO).
venice.silver_lion_grand_jury.source: 67th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.silver_lion_best_director.note: Álex de la Iglesia's first Silver Lion for Best Director — Spanish filmmaker. Same film took Best Screenplay (also Iglesia) — same-film cross-honour and same-recipient double-honour.
venice.silver_lion_best_director.source: 67th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.volpi_cup_best_actor.note: Vincent Gallo's first Volpi Cup for Best Actor — same film took Grand Jury Prize (Skolimowski) at the same ceremony.
venice.volpi_cup_best_actor.source: 67th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.volpi_cup_best_actress.note: Ariane Labed's first Volpi Cup for Best Actress — Greek actress's debut prize.
venice.volpi_cup_best_actress.source: 67th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.best_screenplay.note: Álex de la Iglesia takes Best Screenplay for the same film he won the Silver Lion Best Director for — exceptional same-recipient cross-category double-honour.
venice.best_screenplay.source: 67th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.marcello_mastroianni_award.note: Mila Kunis's first major acting prize — for Aronofsky's Black Swan, two years after Aronofsky's Golden Lion for The Wrestler (2008). Black Swan went on to receive five Academy Award nominations at cer 83 including Best Picture and Best Actress for Natalie Portman (who won).
venice.marcello_mastroianni_award.source: 67th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.lion_of_the_future.source: 67th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.golden_lion.note: Alexander Sokurov's first Golden Lion — Russian auteur's most-prominent international prize. Faust is the final part of Sokurov's tetralogy on the corrupting nature of power (after Moloch, Taurus, and The Sun).
venice.golden_lion.source: 68th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.silver_lion_grand_jury.note: Emanuele Crialese's second major Venice prize — he previously won the Special Jury Prize in 2002 for Respiro. Italian filmmaker's drama about migration to Lampedusa.
venice.silver_lion_grand_jury.source: 68th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.silver_lion_best_director.note: Cai Shangjun's first Silver Lion for Best Director — Chinese filmmaker's revenge drama based on a real-life murder case.
venice.silver_lion_best_director.source: 68th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.volpi_cup_best_actor.note: Michael Fassbender's first Volpi Cup for Best Actor — for Steve McQueen's Shame. McQueen and Fassbender's collaboration continued with 12 Years a Slave (Best Picture Oscar at cer 86).
venice.volpi_cup_best_actor.source: 68th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.volpi_cup_best_actress.note: Deanie Ip's first Volpi Cup for Best Actress — Hong Kong actress's career-late prize at age 64. Ann Hui directed.
venice.volpi_cup_best_actress.source: 68th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.best_screenplay.note: Yorgos Lanthimos and Efthimis Filippou's first major Venice prize — Lanthimos went on to win the Venice Grand Jury Prize in 2018 (The Favourite) and the Golden Lion in 2023 (Poor Things).
venice.best_screenplay.source: 68th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.marcello_mastroianni_award.note: Two-person team award. Shota Sometani and Fumi Nikaido share the Marcello Mastroianni Award for the same film, Sion Sono's Himizu (post-tsunami drama). Both rows correctly flagged co_winner=true.
venice.marcello_mastroianni_award.source: 68th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.lion_of_the_future.source: 68th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.golden_lion.note: Kim Ki-duk becomes the first South Korean filmmaker to win the Golden Lion. Pietà is a violent revenge drama set in a Seoul slum. The win predates Bong Joon-ho's 2019 Cannes Palme d'Or for Parasite by seven years — South Korea's first major-festival top prize of the modern era.
venice.golden_lion.source: 69th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.silver_lion_grand_jury.note: Ulrich Seidl's first major Venice prize — middle entry of his 'Paradise' trilogy.
venice.silver_lion_grand_jury.source: 69th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.silver_lion_best_director.note: Paul Thomas Anderson's first Silver Lion for Best Director — and his only major Venice prize. PTA had previously shared Cannes Best Director in 2002 for Punch-Drunk Love. Same film took the Volpi Cup for Best Actor (shared by Hoffman + Phoenix) at the same ceremony — exceptional same-film cross-honour.
venice.silver_lion_best_director.source: 69th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.volpi_cup_best_actor.note: Two-way team award. Philip Seymour Hoffman and Joaquin Phoenix share the Volpi Cup for Best Actor for the same film, PTA's The Master. Same film also took Silver Lion for Best Director (PT Anderson) — exceptional same-film triple-honour. Hoffman died in 2014; the Volpi was one of his last major prizes. Both rows correctly flagged co_winner=true.
venice.volpi_cup_best_actor.source: 69th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.volpi_cup_best_actress.note: Hadas Yaron's first Volpi Cup for Best Actress — Israeli actress's drama set in the Hasidic Jewish community of Tel Aviv.
venice.volpi_cup_best_actress.source: 69th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.best_screenplay.note: Olivier Assayas's first Venice Best Screenplay — French auteur's autobiographical drama about post-1968 student activism.
venice.best_screenplay.source: 69th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.marcello_mastroianni_award.source: 69th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.lion_of_the_future.note: Turkey's first Lion of the Future. Ali Aydın's debut feature.
venice.lion_of_the_future.source: 69th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.golden_lion.note: Gianfranco Rosi's first Golden Lion — and the FIRST documentary to win the Golden Lion in modern Venice history (the previous documentary Golden Lion was the 1956 winner Below the Sahara — a 57-year gap). Rosi went on to take Venice's Special Jury Prize in 2025 for Below the Clouds. Sacro GRA is a portrait of life around Rome's ring road.
venice.golden_lion.source: 70th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.silver_lion_grand_jury.note: Tsai Ming-liang's first Venice Grand Jury Prize — Taiwanese auteur's contemplative slow-cinema film. Tsai had won Venice's Best Director in 1994 for Vive L'Amour.
venice.silver_lion_grand_jury.source: 70th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.silver_lion_best_director.note: Alexandros Avranas's first Silver Lion for Best Director — Greek filmmaker's family drama. Same film also took the Volpi Cup for Best Actor (Themis Panou) — same-film cross-honour.
venice.silver_lion_best_director.source: 70th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.special_jury_prize.note: Philip Gröning's first Venice Special Jury Prize.
venice.special_jury_prize.source: 70th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.volpi_cup_best_actor.note: Themis Panou's first major acting prize — same film took Silver Lion for Best Director (Avranas) at the same ceremony.
venice.volpi_cup_best_actor.source: 70th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.volpi_cup_best_actress.note: Elena Cotta's first Volpi Cup for Best Actress at age 82 — Italian actress's career-late prize.
venice.volpi_cup_best_actress.source: 70th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.best_screenplay.note: Steve Coogan and Jeff Pope's first Venice Best Screenplay. Philomena went on to receive a Best Adapted Screenplay nomination at the 86th Academy Awards (cer 86) and four other Oscar nominations including Best Picture.
venice.best_screenplay.source: 70th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.marcello_mastroianni_award.source: 70th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.lion_of_the_future.source: 70th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.golden_lion.winner_film: A Pigeon Sat on a Branch Reflecting on Existence
venice.golden_lion.note: Roy Andersson's first Golden Lion — Swedish auteur's deadpan tableaux style. The film is the third part of his 'Living trilogy' (after Songs from the Second Floor and You, the Living). Andersson later took Silver Lion for Best Director in 2019 for About Endlessness.
venice.golden_lion.source: 71st Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.silver_lion_grand_jury.note: Joshua Oppenheimer's first Venice Grand Jury Prize — companion piece to The Act of Killing (2012). Both films document the 1965-66 Indonesian killings. The Look of Silence went on to receive a Best Documentary Feature Oscar nomination at cer 88.
venice.silver_lion_grand_jury.source: 71st Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.silver_lion_best_director.note: Andrei Konchalovsky's first Silver Lion for Best Director — he later shared Silver Lion for Best Director in 2016 (Paradise) and won Special Jury Prize in 2020 (Dear Comrades!), making him a three-time Venice prizewinner across the decade.
venice.silver_lion_best_director.source: 71st Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.special_jury_prize.source: 71st Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.volpi_cup_best_actor.note: Adam Driver's first Volpi Cup for Best Actor — pre-Star Wars career milestone.
venice.volpi_cup_best_actor.source: 71st Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.volpi_cup_best_actress.note: Alba Rohrwacher's first Volpi Cup for Best Actress. Same film also took Volpi for Best Actor (Adam Driver) — exceptional same-film acting double-honour.
venice.volpi_cup_best_actress.source: 71st Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.best_screenplay.source: 71st Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.marcello_mastroianni_award.source: 71st Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.lion_of_the_future.note: Chaitanya Tamhane's debut feature wins Lion of the Future — Indian filmmaker's first major prize. Tamhane went on to win Best Screenplay at Venice 2020 for The Disciple.
venice.lion_of_the_future.source: 71st Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.golden_lion.note: Lorenzo Vigas's debut feature wins the Golden Lion — Venezuela's first Golden Lion. Exceptionally rare for a debut feature to take Venice's top prize.
venice.golden_lion.source: 72nd Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.silver_lion_grand_jury.note: Charlie Kaufman and Duke Johnson's stop-motion animated feature wins the Grand Jury Prize — exceptionally rare for an animated film to take a major Venice prize. Anomalisa went on to receive a Best Animated Feature nomination at the 88th Academy Awards (cer 88).
venice.silver_lion_grand_jury.source: 72nd Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.silver_lion_best_director.note: Pablo Trapero's first Silver Lion for Best Director — Argentine filmmaker's true-crime drama based on the Puccio family kidnappings.
venice.silver_lion_best_director.source: 72nd Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.special_jury_prize.source: 72nd Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.volpi_cup_best_actor.note: Fabrice Luchini's first Volpi Cup for Best Actor — French actor's career-late prize. Same film also took Best Screenplay (Christian Vincent) — same-film cross-honour.
venice.volpi_cup_best_actor.source: 72nd Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.volpi_cup_best_actress.note: Valeria Golino's first Volpi Cup for Best Actress — Italian actress.
venice.volpi_cup_best_actress.source: 72nd Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.best_screenplay.source: 72nd Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.marcello_mastroianni_award.note: Abraham Attah's debut acting role wins the Marcello Mastroianni Award — Ghanaian child actor in Cary Joji Fukunaga's drama about child soldiers in West Africa.
venice.marcello_mastroianni_award.source: 72nd Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.lion_of_the_future.note: Brady Corbet's directorial debut wins Lion of the Future — Corbet later took Venice's Silver Lion for Best Director in 2024 for The Brutalist (which went on to 3 Oscars at cer 97 including Brody Best Actor). Two Venice prizes nine years apart for Corbet, with the second representing the prestige escalation. CSV film_title contains a wikitext markup leak (''The Childhood of a Leader'' with italic markers) — Phase E winner_film passes via fuzzy substring match. See known-issue venice-lion-of-the-future-italic-leak-2026-05-04.
venice.lion_of_the_future.source: 72nd Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.golden_lion.note: Lav Diaz becomes the first Filipino filmmaker to win the Golden Lion. The Woman Who Left is part of his career-long catalogue of long-form, slow-cinema epics; the win marked the highest international honour for Filipino cinema in modern festival history.
venice.golden_lion.source: 73rd Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.silver_lion_grand_jury.note: Tom Ford's first major Venice prize — fashion-designer-turned-filmmaker's second feature.
venice.silver_lion_grand_jury.source: 73rd Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.silver_lion_best_director.note: Two-way tie. Amat Escalante (The Untamed) shares Silver Lion for Best Director with Andrei Konchalovsky (Paradise). Escalante had previously won Cannes Best Director 2013 for Heli; Konchalovsky later won Venice's Special Jury Prize in 2020 for Dear Comrades! Both rows correctly flagged co_winner=true.
venice.silver_lion_best_director.source: 73rd Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.special_jury_prize.source: 73rd Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.volpi_cup_best_actor.note: Oscar Martínez's first Volpi Cup for Best Actor — Argentine actor's most-prominent international prize.
venice.volpi_cup_best_actor.source: 73rd Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.volpi_cup_best_actress.note: Emma Stone's first Volpi Cup for Best Actress — she went on to win the Best Actress Oscar at the 89th Academy Awards (cer 89) for the same role. The Venice-to-Oscar acting pipeline played out cleanly.
venice.volpi_cup_best_actress.source: 73rd Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.best_screenplay.source: 73rd Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.marcello_mastroianni_award.note: Paula Beer's first major acting prize.
venice.marcello_mastroianni_award.source: 73rd Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.lion_of_the_future.source: 73rd Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.golden_lion.note: Guillermo del Toro's first Golden Lion — The Shape of Water went on to win four Academy Awards at the 90th ceremony (cer 90), including Best Picture and Best Director, making del Toro the third Mexican director to win Best Director Oscar in five years (after Cuarón 2014 and Iñárritu 2015 + 2016 — the 'Three Amigos' era of Mexican cinema).
venice.golden_lion.source: 74th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.silver_lion_grand_jury.note: Samuel Maoz's second Venice prize — he previously won the Golden Lion in 2009 for Lebanon.
venice.silver_lion_grand_jury.source: 74th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.silver_lion_best_director.note: Xavier Legrand's debut feature wins BOTH Silver Lion for Best Director AND Lion of the Future at the same ceremony — an exceptional same-film double-honour for a first feature, on the same scale as Saint Omer (Diop 2022).
venice.silver_lion_best_director.source: 74th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.special_jury_prize.note: Warwick Thornton's second major festival prize — he had previously won the Caméra d'Or at Cannes 2009 (Samson and Delilah). Indigenous Australian filmmaker.
venice.special_jury_prize.source: 74th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.volpi_cup_best_actor.note: Kamel El Basha becomes the first Palestinian actor to win the Volpi Cup for Best Actor.
venice.volpi_cup_best_actor.source: 74th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.volpi_cup_best_actress.note: Charlotte Rampling's first Volpi Cup for Best Actress.
venice.volpi_cup_best_actress.source: 74th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.best_screenplay.note: Martin McDonagh's first Venice Best Screenplay — Three Billboards went on to win Best Actress (McDormand) and Best Supporting Actor (Rockwell) at the 90th Academy Awards (cer 90). McDonagh later won Venice Best Screenplay AGAIN in 2022 for The Banshees of Inisherin.
venice.best_screenplay.source: 74th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.marcello_mastroianni_award.source: 74th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.lion_of_the_future.note: Xavier Legrand's debut feature Custody wins Lion of the Future / Luigi De Laurentiis Award — same film also took Silver Lion for Best Director at the same ceremony, an exceptional same-film cross-honour.
venice.lion_of_the_future.source: 74th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.golden_lion.note: Alfonso Cuarón's first Golden Lion — and the first Netflix-distributed film to win Venice's top prize. Cuarón went on to win Best Director, Best Cinematography, and Best International Feature at the 91st Academy Awards (cer 91) for Roma — a clean Venice-to-Oscar trajectory. Cuarón is the first Mexican filmmaker to win Venice's Golden Lion.
venice.golden_lion.source: 75th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.silver_lion_grand_jury.note: Yorgos Lanthimos's first Venice Grand Jury Prize — he later took the Golden Lion in 2023 for Poor Things. Same film also took the Volpi Cup for Best Actress (Olivia Colman) at the same ceremony.
venice.silver_lion_grand_jury.source: 75th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.silver_lion_best_director.note: Jacques Audiard's first Venice Best Director — French filmmaker's first English-language feature. Audiard had previously won the Cannes Palme d'Or in 2015 for Dheepan and the Cannes Grand Prix in 2009 for A Prophet.
venice.silver_lion_best_director.source: 75th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.special_jury_prize.note: Jennifer Kent's second feature wins the Special Jury Prize. Same film also took the Marcello Mastroianni Award (Baykali Ganambarr) at the same ceremony.
venice.special_jury_prize.source: 75th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.volpi_cup_best_actor.note: Willem Dafoe's first Volpi Cup for Best Actor — for portraying Vincent van Gogh. Dafoe went on to receive a Best Actor nomination at the 91st Academy Awards (cer 91) for the same role.
venice.volpi_cup_best_actor.source: 75th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.volpi_cup_best_actress.note: Olivia Colman's first Volpi Cup for Best Actress — she went on to win the Best Actress Oscar at the 91st Academy Awards (cer 91) for the same role (Queen Anne in The Favourite). The Venice-to-Oscar acting pipeline played out cleanly. Same film also took Grand Jury Prize (Lanthimos) at the same ceremony — same-film cross-honour.
venice.volpi_cup_best_actress.source: 75th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.best_screenplay.note: The Coen brothers' first Venice Best Screenplay — they had previously won the Cannes Palme d'Or for Barton Fink (1991) and the Cannes Best Director for Fargo (1996). The Ballad of Buster Scruggs received a Best Adapted Screenplay Oscar nomination at the 91st ceremony (cer 91).
venice.best_screenplay.source: 75th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.marcello_mastroianni_award.note: Baykali Ganambarr's first major acting prize — Indigenous Australian actor in his debut feature role. Same film took the Special Jury Prize (Kent) at the same ceremony.
venice.marcello_mastroianni_award.source: 75th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.lion_of_the_future.winner_film: The Day I Lost My Shadow
venice.lion_of_the_future.note: Syria's first Lion of the Future. Soudade Kaadan's debut feature.
venice.lion_of_the_future.source: 75th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.golden_lion.note: Joker becomes the first comic-book film to win the Golden Lion in Venice's history. Todd Phillips's first major festival prize. The film went on to receive eleven Academy Award nominations at cer 92, with Joaquin Phoenix winning Best Actor (the second consecutive Best Actor win for portraying the Joker character — after Heath Ledger's posthumous Best Supporting Actor at cer 81).
venice.golden_lion.source: 76th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.silver_lion_grand_jury.note: Roman Polanski's third major Venice prize (after the Golden Lion for The Pianist at Cannes 2002 — wait, Pianist won Cannes Palme not Venice. Polanski's prior Venice prize was the Career Golden Lion at the 51st festival, 1994). The 2019 Grand Jury Prize was widely controversial given Polanski's status as a fugitive from US justice; Polanski did not attend.
venice.silver_lion_grand_jury.source: 76th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.silver_lion_best_director.note: Roy Andersson's second major Venice prize — he previously won the Golden Lion in 2014 for A Pigeon Sat on a Branch Reflecting on Existence. Swedish auteur's deadpan tableaux style.
venice.silver_lion_best_director.source: 76th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.special_jury_prize.winner_film: The Mafia Is No Longer What It Used to Be
venice.special_jury_prize.source: 76th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.volpi_cup_best_actor.note: Luca Marinelli's first Volpi Cup for Best Actor — Italian actor's most-prominent international prize.
venice.volpi_cup_best_actor.source: 76th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.volpi_cup_best_actress.note: Ariane Ascaride's first Volpi Cup for Best Actress — French actress and frequent Robert Guédiguian collaborator.
venice.volpi_cup_best_actress.source: 76th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.best_screenplay.note: Hong Kong filmmaker Yonfan's animated feature wins Best Screenplay — exceptionally rare for an animated film to take a screenplay prize at a major festival.
venice.best_screenplay.source: 76th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.marcello_mastroianni_award.source: 76th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.lion_of_the_future.note: Sudan's first Lion of the Future. Amjad Abu Alala's debut feature — also Sudan's first submission for the Best International Feature Oscar.
venice.lion_of_the_future.source: 76th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.golden_lion.note: Chloé Zhao becomes the first Asian woman ever to win the Golden Lion. Nomadland went on to win Best Picture, Best Director, and Best Actress (Frances McDormand) at the 93rd Academy Awards (cer 93) — Zhao becoming the second woman ever and the first woman of colour to win Best Director Oscar. The Venice-to-Oscar Best Picture pipeline is exceptionally rare; Nomadland's trajectory remains one of the cleanest in modern festival history. The 77th Venice ran on a COVID-restricted format with reduced awards (Lion of the Future not awarded — see era_override).
venice.golden_lion.source: 77th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.silver_lion_grand_jury.note: Michel Franco's first major Venice prize — Mexican filmmaker's politically-charged dystopian drama.
venice.silver_lion_grand_jury.source: 77th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.silver_lion_best_director.note: Kiyoshi Kurosawa's first Silver Lion for Best Director — Japanese auteur's WWII espionage drama.
venice.silver_lion_best_director.source: 77th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.special_jury_prize.note: Andrei Konchalovsky's second Venice prize in five years — he previously shared the Silver Lion for Best Director in 2016 for Paradise.
venice.special_jury_prize.source: 77th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.volpi_cup_best_actor.note: Pierfrancesco Favino's first Volpi Cup for Best Actor — Italian actor.
venice.volpi_cup_best_actor.source: 77th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.volpi_cup_best_actress.note: Vanessa Kirby's first Volpi Cup for Best Actress — she went on to receive a Best Actress nomination at the 93rd Academy Awards (cer 93) for the same role.
venice.volpi_cup_best_actress.source: 77th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.best_screenplay.note: Chaitanya Tamhane's second feature wins Best Screenplay — he previously won the Caméra d'Or at Cannes 2014 for Court (his debut feature).
venice.best_screenplay.source: 77th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.marcello_mastroianni_award.note: Iranian child actor Rouhollah Zamani's first major acting prize.
venice.marcello_mastroianni_award.source: 77th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.golden_lion.note: Audrey Diwan's first Golden Lion — for L'événement (Happening), an adaptation of Annie Ernaux's autobiographical novel about a young woman seeking an illegal abortion in 1960s France. Diwan's second feature.
venice.golden_lion.source: 78th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.silver_lion_grand_jury.note: Paolo Sorrentino's first Grand Jury Prize at Venice — his autobiographical Naples-set drama. The Hand of God went on to receive a Best International Feature nomination at the 94th Academy Awards (cer 94). Same film also took the Marcello Mastroianni Award (Filippo Scotti).
venice.silver_lion_grand_jury.source: 78th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.silver_lion_best_director.note: Jane Campion's first Silver Lion for Best Director — she went on to win Best Director at the 94th Academy Awards (cer 94) for the same film, becoming only the third woman ever to win Best Director Oscar. Power of the Dog's Venice-to-Oscar trajectory was one of the most-discussed awards seasons of the era.
venice.silver_lion_best_director.source: 78th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.special_jury_prize.note: Michelangelo Frammartino's first major Venice prize — Italian filmmaker's contemplative documentary-fiction hybrid about cave exploration in 1960s Calabria.
venice.special_jury_prize.source: 78th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.volpi_cup_best_actor.note: John Arcilla becomes the first Filipino actor to win the Volpi Cup for Best Actor.
venice.volpi_cup_best_actor.source: 78th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.volpi_cup_best_actress.note: Penélope Cruz's first Volpi Cup for Best Actress — for Pedro Almodóvar's Parallel Mothers. Cruz had previously won the Cannes Best Supporting Actress shared 6-way for Almodóvar's Volver in 2006. Almodóvar himself later won Venice's Golden Lion in 2024 (The Room Next Door).
venice.volpi_cup_best_actress.source: 78th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.best_screenplay.note: Maggie Gyllenhaal's first Cannes-equivalent prize — won Best Screenplay for her directorial debut, an adaptation of Elena Ferrante's novel. Gyllenhaal received Oscar nominations for Adapted Screenplay and Picture at cer 94.
venice.best_screenplay.source: 78th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.marcello_mastroianni_award.note: Filippo Scotti's first major acting prize — Sorrentino's autobiographical lead. Same film took Grand Jury Prize at the same ceremony.
venice.marcello_mastroianni_award.source: 78th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.lion_of_the_future.note: Romanian co-directed debut feature wins Lion of the Future / Luigi De Laurentiis Award.
venice.lion_of_the_future.source: 78th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.golden_lion.winner_film: All the Beauty and the Bloodshed
venice.golden_lion.note: Laura Poitras's first Golden Lion — only the second documentary in modern Venice history to win the top prize (after Gianfranco Rosi's Sacro GRA, 2013). The film centres on photographer Nan Goldin's activism against the Sackler family. Poitras had previously won the Best Documentary Oscar for Citizenfour (cer 87).
venice.golden_lion.source: 79th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.silver_lion_grand_jury.note: Alice Diop's debut feature wins the Grand Jury Prize AND the Lion of the Future at the same ceremony — an exceptional same-film double-honour for a first feature. Saint Omer went on to be France's submission for Best International Feature at cer 95.
venice.silver_lion_grand_jury.source: 79th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.silver_lion_best_director.note: Luca Guadagnino's first Silver Lion for Best Director — same film also took the Marcello Mastroianni Award (Taylor Russell) at the same ceremony. Guadagnino had previously been Oscar-nominated as a producer for Call Me by Your Name (cer 90).
venice.silver_lion_best_director.source: 79th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.special_jury_prize.note: Jafar Panahi's first Venice Special Jury Prize — Panahi was imprisoned in Iran at the time and could not attend. The film centres on a director shooting a film through proxies due to a travel ban — directly autobiographical. Panahi later won the Palme d'Or in 2025 (It Was Just an Accident), his first festival appearance after the lifting of his travel ban.
venice.special_jury_prize.source: 79th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.volpi_cup_best_actor.note: Colin Farrell's first major acting prize — went on to receive Best Actor nominations at the 95th Academy Awards (cer 95) and BAFTA. Same film also took Best Screenplay (McDonagh) at the same ceremony.
venice.volpi_cup_best_actor.source: 79th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.volpi_cup_best_actress.note: Cate Blanchett's first Volpi Cup for Best Actress — her career-late renaissance under Todd Field's direction. Blanchett went on to receive a Best Actress nomination at the 95th Academy Awards (cer 95) for the same role; her sixth acting Oscar nomination.
venice.volpi_cup_best_actress.source: 79th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.best_screenplay.note: Martin McDonagh's first Venice Best Screenplay — went on to receive a Best Original Screenplay Oscar nomination at cer 95. Same film took Best Actor (Farrell) at the same ceremony.
venice.best_screenplay.source: 79th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.marcello_mastroianni_award.note: Taylor Russell's first major acting prize — Bones and All took Best Director (Guadagnino) at the same ceremony.
venice.marcello_mastroianni_award.source: 79th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.lion_of_the_future.note: Alice Diop's debut feature Saint Omer wins Lion of the Future / Luigi De Laurentiis — same film also took the Grand Jury Prize at the same ceremony. The same-film Grand-Jury + Lion-of-the-Future double is exceptionally rare in Venice's history.
venice.lion_of_the_future.source: 79th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.golden_lion.note: Yorgos Lanthimos's first Golden Lion — Poor Things went on to win four Academy Awards at cer 96 (Best Actress for Emma Stone, Production Design, Costume Design, Makeup & Hairstyling) and the BAFTA Best Film. The film's Venice-to-Oscar trajectory was one of the most-celebrated of the season.
venice.golden_lion.source: 80th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.silver_lion_grand_jury.note: Ryusuke Hamaguchi's first Venice Grand Jury Prize — Hamaguchi had previously won Cannes Best Screenplay in 2021 for Drive My Car (which went on to win the International Feature Oscar at cer 94).
venice.silver_lion_grand_jury.source: 80th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.silver_lion_best_director.note: Matteo Garrone's first Silver Lion for Best Director — Garrone had previously won two Cannes Grand Prix wins (Gomorrah, 2008; Reality, 2012). Same film also took the Marcello Mastroianni Award (Seydou Sarr) at the same ceremony.
venice.silver_lion_best_director.source: 80th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.special_jury_prize.note: Agnieszka Holland's first Venice Special Jury Prize — Polish veteran filmmaker's drama set on the Belarus-Poland border refugee crisis. The win was politically charged in Poland; the ruling government condemned it publicly.
venice.special_jury_prize.source: 80th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.volpi_cup_best_actor.note: Peter Sarsgaard's first major acting prize — for Michel Franco's Memory, opposite Jessica Chastain.
venice.volpi_cup_best_actor.source: 80th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.volpi_cup_best_actress.note: Cailee Spaeny's first major acting prize — for Sofia Coppola's Priscilla, portraying Priscilla Presley.
venice.volpi_cup_best_actress.source: 80th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.best_screenplay.source: 80th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.marcello_mastroianni_award.note: Seydou Sarr's debut acting role wins the Marcello Mastroianni Award — Senegalese actor's first major prize. Same film took Best Director (Garrone) at the same ceremony.
venice.marcello_mastroianni_award.source: 80th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.lion_of_the_future.note: Lee Hong-chi's debut feature wins Lion of the Future — Taiwanese-Hong Kong co-production.
venice.lion_of_the_future.source: 80th Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.golden_lion.note: Pedro Almodóvar's first Golden Lion — and his first English-language feature. Almodóvar had previously won three Cannes prizes including Best Original Screenplay for Talk to Her (cer 75) and Best Director for All About My Mother (cer 72). The Room Next Door's win marked the first time a Spanish filmmaker had taken Venice's top prize.
venice.golden_lion.source: 81st Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.silver_lion_grand_jury.note: Maura Delpero's second feature wins the Grand Jury Prize. Delpero became Italy's submission for the 97th Academy Awards Best International Feature (cer 97).
venice.silver_lion_grand_jury.source: 81st Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.silver_lion_best_director.note: Brady Corbet's first Silver Lion for Best Director — The Brutalist subsequently won three Academy Awards at cer 97 (Best Actor for Adrien Brody, Best Cinematography, Best Original Score). The Venice Best Director-to-Oscar Best Actor trajectory was one of the season's defining narratives.
venice.silver_lion_best_director.source: 81st Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.special_jury_prize.note: Dea Kulumbegashvili's second feature wins the Special Jury Prize — Georgian filmmaker's continued Venice presence after Beginning's 2020 acclaim.
venice.special_jury_prize.source: 81st Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.volpi_cup_best_actor.note: Vincent Lindon's first Volpi Cup for Best Actor — Lindon had previously won Cannes Best Actor in 2015 for The Measure of a Man, making him a winner of both major continental Best Actor prizes.
venice.volpi_cup_best_actor.source: 81st Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.volpi_cup_best_actress.note: Nicole Kidman's first Volpi Cup for Best Actress — Kidman had previously won the Oscar for Best Actress (The Hours, cer 75) and the Cannes Best Actress (To Die For, 1995 — outside our scope).
venice.volpi_cup_best_actress.source: 81st Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.best_screenplay.note: Murilo Hauser and Heitor Lorega's first major screenplay prize — I'm Still Here went on to win the 97th Academy Awards Best International Feature (cer 97) — Brazil's first International Feature Oscar.
venice.best_screenplay.source: 81st Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.marcello_mastroianni_award.source: 81st Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.lion_of_the_future.note: Sarah Friedland's debut feature wins Lion of the Future. Friedland also took Best Director in the Orizzonti section at the same ceremony — a rare cross-section honour.
venice.lion_of_the_future.source: 81st Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.golden_lion.note: Jim Jarmusch's first Golden Lion — American indie auteur's most-prominent career-late prize. Jarmusch had previously won the Cannes Grand Prix in 2005 for Broken Flowers and the Caméra d'Or for Stranger Than Paradise (1984, outside scope).
venice.golden_lion.source: 82nd Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.silver_lion_grand_jury.note: Kaouther Ben Hania's first Venice Grand Jury Prize — Tunisian filmmaker's politically-charged docudrama about the killing of a Palestinian child during the 2024 Gaza war.
venice.silver_lion_grand_jury.source: 82nd Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.silver_lion_best_director.note: Benny Safdie's first Silver Lion for Best Director — solo directorial effort following the Safdie brothers' break-up of their joint directorial partnership (Uncut Gems, Good Time).
venice.silver_lion_best_director.source: 82nd Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.special_jury_prize.note: Gianfranco Rosi's second major Venice prize — he previously won the Golden Lion for Sacro GRA in 2013, the first documentary to win the top prize in modern Venice history.
venice.special_jury_prize.source: 82nd Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.volpi_cup_best_actor.note: Toni Servillo's first Volpi Cup for Best Actor — Italian veteran best known for collaborations with Paolo Sorrentino (The Great Beauty won Best International Feature at cer 86).
venice.volpi_cup_best_actor.source: 82nd Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.volpi_cup_best_actress.winner_film: The Sun Rises on Us All
venice.volpi_cup_best_actress.note: Xin Zhilei becomes the first Chinese actress to win the Volpi Cup for Best Actress.
venice.volpi_cup_best_actress.source: 82nd Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.best_screenplay.source: 82nd Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.marcello_mastroianni_award.source: 82nd Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org

venice.lion_of_the_future.source: 82nd Venice Film Festival Wikipedia article / labiennale.org
